title: Netcat (nc)
description: The TCP/IP Swiss Army knife — read and write raw data across network connections.
fullExplanation: Netcat (nc) is a simple but powerful networking utility that reads and writes data across TCP or UDP connections. It can act as a client or server, making it useful for port scanning, banner grabbing, file transfer, creating backdoors, and debugging network services. Often called the 'Swiss Army knife of networking'. ncat (from Nmap) and socat are modern alternatives with SSL support.

useCases.description: Connect to port 80, send HTTP request manually
Listen on port 4444 — receive reverse shell
Wait for victim to connect back

keyFacts: nc -e flag (execute) often disabled in modern distros for security
ncat (Nmap): modern replacement with SSL, IPv6, proxy support
socat: more powerful alternative — SSL, serial ports, Unix sockets
Netcat doesn't encrypt — use ncat --ssl or socat for encrypted channels
Firewall evasion: use common ports (80, 443) for reverse shells
